title: DSCI Senior Resident Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for 34 Posts
short_info: Delhi State Cancer Institutes (DSCI) Senior Resident Notification 2026 has been released on the official website. A total of 34 vacancies have been announced through the DSCI Senior Resident Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 45 years. Age relaxation will be as per government rules.

qualification.education_qualification: Recognized MBBS qualification, Recognized PG (MD/DNB) in concerned field, Registration with Delhi Medical Council
qualification.details: Clinical Oncology / Radiotherapy: Recognized MBBS qualification. Recognized PG (MD/DNB) in Concerned field. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Onco-Anaesthesia: Recognized MBBS qualification. Post Graduate qualification like (MD / DNB / DA) in Concerned field. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Intensive & Critical Care: Recognized MBBS qualification. Post Graduate qualification like (MD / DNB / DA) in Concerned Field. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Nuclear Medicine: Recognized MBBS qualification. Recognized PG (MD/ DNB/Diploma) in Nuclear Medicine. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Onco-Pathology: Recognized MBBS qualification. Recognized PG (MD / DNB) in concerned field. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Lab Medicine (Lab Medicine / Microbiology/ Biochemistry): Recognized MBBS qualification. Recognized PG (MD / DNB) in concerned field. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Onco-Imaging / Radiodiagnosis: Recognized MBBS qualification. Recognized Post Graduate Degree / Diploma (MD / DNB / DMRD) in Concerned field. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Surgical Oncology (ENT): Recognized MBBS qualification. Recognized PG (MS/ MD/ DNB) in ENT. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Gastroenterology: Recognized MBBS qualification. Recognized PG (MD/DNB) in Medicine. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Internal Medicine: Recognized MBBS qualification. Recognized PG (MD / DNB) in concerned field. Registration with Delhi Medical Council. Preventive Oncology & Onco-Epidemiology: Recognized MBBS qualification. Recognized PG (MD/DNB) in Preventive & Social Medicine. Registration with Delhi Medical Council.

selection_process: Personal Interview Written Exam (if more than 5 candidates per discipline)

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to attend the walk-in interview for DSCI Senior Resident posts?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview for the Senior Resident posts at Delhi State Cancer Institutes is scheduled for 29th May 2026. Candidates should ensure they report to the venue on this date.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for the Senior Resident post?
faq.answer: There are a total of 34 vacancies announced for the Senior Resident position across various specializations at Delhi State Cancer Institutes.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for the Senior Resident posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a recognized MBBS qualification and a recognized PG (MD/DNB) in the concerned field, along with registration with the Delhi Medical Council.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for applying to these positions?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for candidates applying for the Senior Resident posts is 45 years. Age relaxation will be applicable as per government norms.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Senior Resident position?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 67700 per month, along with applicable allowances.

faq.question: Q6. Is there an application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee required for candidates applying under any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for the Senior Resident posts?
faq.answer: The selection process will primarily involve a Personal Interview. A Written Exam may be conducted if more than 5 candidates apply for a specific discipline.

faq.question: Q8. How should candidates apply for the Senior Resident positions?
faq.answer: This is a walk-in recruitment. Eligible candidates need to report directly at the specified venue on the interview date with all required documents.

faq.question: Q9. Where will the walk-in interview be held?
faq.answer: The official notification should be checked for the exact venue details. However, it is generally held at the Delhi State Cancer Institutes premises.

faq.question: Q10. What documents should candidates bring for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must bring original and self-attested photocopies of their educational certificates, registration, ID proof, and any other relevant documents as specified in the notification.
